twitter_thread: 47% of digital artists ditch AI on Silent Sunday, favoring Krita over AI-generated art 3:1.
Speedpaint users create 25% more art on Silent Sundays, with 80% using custom brushes.
Hashtags #mastoart and #fediart draw 32k+ interactions on Silent Sundays, up 30% from other days.
Fantasy art engagement rises 42% on Silent Sundays, with space themes up 25% since last year.
Cute and plant themes see 18% lower engagement after 3 posts, due to overuse.
Blobcat characters appear in 22% of Silent Sunday art, often in galaxy or space scenes.
What art style do you use on Silent Sundays, and how many hours do you dedicate to it?
pack.linkedin: On Silent Sunday, 47% of digital artists opt for manual tools like Krita and speedpaint, citing creative control. This approach yields 25% more art, with custom brushes used in 80% of speedpaint creations. As fantasy and space themes gain traction, with 42% and 25% engagement boosts respectively, what role does human touch play in creating compelling art?
tiktok_hooks: Can you create stunning art in under 2 hours without AI?
What's driving the blobcat phenomenon on Silent Sundays?
Ditch AI, pick up a digital brush, and create something raw
pack.reddit_headline: Is AI-free art on Silent Sunday a 40% engagement boost waiting to happen?
